deck_excerpt: Magnet Warrior with a new Genesys points list
deck_description:
---------------Comments on the cards in the deck:

 

Utopic Zexal is insane in mirror matches; it prevents all targeting interactions your opponent might have, especially Tellusion, and steals monsters that would be used for Fusion Summon. The only catch is that you need to get rid of Plasma Magnum so that your opponent's Tellusion can't take your Utopic Future.

 

And if you steal Beta, you can use its effect to bring a Magnet Warrior from the deck. If you use the Tellusion effect in the Battle Phase and bring Sigma Plus, along with Utopic Zexal on the field (being LIGHT, without the effects of Plasma Magnum), it becomes insane because the opponent won't even be able to declare an attack.

 

Also, when using Flux's effect on opponents turn, don't chain Beta because you'll end up removing all your monsters from the deck for no reason.

 

Remember that you could have summoned Linear Magnum and then bounced it to your hand with Flux, guaranteeing a follow-up. This is also useful if you're using cl1 Omega Plus, cl2 Flux, bounce Linear Magnum and an opponent's monster.

 

Misplay regarding not using Beta's effect multiple times on the opponent's turn. It alone can set up Telllusion because the second effect isn't once per turn.

 

You can use the Magnet Bonding eff to Fusion Summon on turn 1 to dodge cards like Impermanence, Veiler, and Mourner; simply chain it and summon a Fusion.

 

Magnet Bonding can only be used during the Main Phase; you can't use it in the Draw Phase to play around Droll & Lock Bird. You also can't OTK using it during the Battle Phase.

 

Linear Magnum Plus Minus targets any EARTH monster on the field to gain ATK, not just your own monsters.

 

Always make the most of Linear Magnum Plus Minus pop with Omega Plus, and as soon as the turn starts, summon Tellusion to return it to the field.

 

You don't need the fusion on the field transforming the monsters into EARTH to activate Omega Plus's effect; you can trigger the effect with Flux, Tellusion, or Beta on your opponent's turn. Make Flux CL1 and Omega Plus CL2.

 

Don't just reborn Omega Plus because you can; banishing it can cost you the game. If you get hit by D.D. Crow on Omega Plus, it will still destroy the monster.

 

Zexal is terrible against decks that can activate many cards in the GY. The Horus and Mitsurugi engines are examples.

 

Decks where the effect of turning everything into Earth Monsters matters: Yummy, Tenpai, Fiendsmith, Krosea lock, Dominus Impulse lock, Voiceless Voice, etc.

 

For Tellusion to be summoned, the monsters on the field need to be face-up. Sigma Plus's effect is continuous during the battle phase; the opponent will have to attack my Earth monsters, even if they don't want to.

 

Always look at the tech cards your opponent uses and play around them. When fusion summon using Sigma Minus and Sigma Plus in hand, always make CL1 Plasma Magnum CL2 Sigma Plus, so you don't get hit by Ash Blossom's effect to search for Magnet Bonding.

 

If you have Charmy in your hand, Normal Summon it to perform a Rank-4 XYZ Summon; you don't want to waste your Normal Summon on Sigma Minus when you want to use the hand's effect for a Fusion Summon.

 

If you use Forbidden Droplet sending Epsilon, Beta, etc., and you don't control any Magnet Warrior monsters at the resolution of Magnet Induction, does its effect resolve? Yes, it Special Summons any other Magnet Warrior monster instead.

 

This means you only need to have one Magnet Warrior to activate the card, but you don't need to control a Magnet Warrior at the resolution of the effect.

 

I only just realized that Linear Magnum sends from the deck, hand, or face-up field to the GY now. I could have summoned it several times and thought I had no way to send the two monsters back because they were in my hand.

 

Telllusion requires monsters to be face-up on the field to use and banish with its effect; the same applies if you destroy a monster on the field with Omega Plus's effect. Tellusion can be summoned back to the field more than once per turn, which is quite interesting in the grind game.

 

You can take Dominus Impulse on Infernal Punisher. A cool play to make with this card is to special summon Duoterion when you already have another one in the GY, so you can make the RANK-5 Vespenato.

 

Against decks that activate important effects in the GY, Zexal cannot activate its effect if the last chain link is not from a card on the table. Even more so if you are playing against Dracotail or Mitsurugi, for example, they can chain block the important effects and you end up not even being able to activate Zexal.

 

Therefore, a good player can play around these cards in every way. And it's not like you're winning games just because Zexal prevents your opponent from targeting other cards on the field.

 

If you have Sigma Plus on the field, your opponent will have to attack your EARTH monsters; they can't attack, for example, a Special Summon, Zexal, or another monster of a different attribute.

 

Sigma Plus can also add cards from the gy to your hand; you don't always have to Special Summon the target you selected. This is great for adding Sigma Minus back to your hand in grind game.

 

It makes sense to put your monsters in attack position because your opponent's monsters should attack your cards, while you have Sigma Plus on the field. This way you get rid of an annoying opponent's monster during their Battle Phase by tagg out Telllusion.

 

The deck's follow-up is nothing if you can't perform a Fusion Summon, so on turn 2 the most important thing is to add Sigma Minus to your hand to ensure you can always perform a Fusion Summon. This is very important because you can't rely on Beta's "add" effect to pass.

 

If your opponent temporarily banishes Omega Plus, which was summoned by its own effect, it doesn't return to the field; it remains banished.

 

If one of Flux's targets leaves the field before its effect resolves, it still bounces the other card that targeted it. So you can perform cl1 Flux cl2 Telllusion and your opponent's card will bounce to their hand normally.

So I know this deck's not great, but I feel like there is something here.  Digital bugs are pretty good at getting into rank-3's, which can can include Cicaida King.  They can also help you find Inzektor's and recur them, and if you find dragonfly and hornet you can spam the field while also dealing with pesky cards your opponent left.  Bug signal lets you tag XYZ's into other forms to avoid disruption and grab useful abilities.  Being able to search Transcicaida for battlewasp is probabally too cute, but I couldn't resist.  That said, it feels super fragile and likely below the genisis power level.  Import and Reborn were largly chosen as I was struggling to come up with more relevent interaction, while also giving you more material.

deck_description:
{Note: This is not an FTK, original idea by me "yeah no one thought about it before me, this deck dates to 3/16/2026}


Usage:


Dark World: Engine for sacred beast's discard cost effect
Sacred Beasts: Main Discard Engine, Material for xyz and the fusion
Extra deck: Super poly and Kaiju for cleansing using venemy and preda and other stuff

Guide:

Simple, each sacred beast of the 3 "+1 the creator" adds a card and discards a card, which triggers dark world, which rebuilds hand and summons monster, also sacred has draw engine (paradise)
you can use hamon to draw, or raviel to add uriel to add trap to fuse and negate
or just keep summoning and xyz or link or whatever, you also will really need the fusion summon quick spell for darkworld, really helpful even with sacred, you can use sacred hamon or creator to go into colossus , also blitzzique searcher card help in...well, searching.



what's so good about this deck? 


>Unaffected by hand traps "Like Mermails", if you can summon fusion grapha at the beginning it will be even better (your only enemy is droll and any banish really like shifter)
>Silvva can shuffle during opponent's turn bec grapha fusion, or destroy cards using wu or grapha.
>Sacred Fusion easy 3 negates alone, can be resummoned with a bao link 4 for additional 3 negates total 6.
>can easily break boards, that's why I don't run handtraps, and dont't try to.
>can bypass many field negates if played correctly or with a good hand, I mean bad plays always suck no matter the deck right?
>can make a really good field going first, like...a really good field
>You can use zombiestein + any card really to negate droplet
>dark world's quick play spell allows for a second fusion of Sacred beasts, which can use "ANY" level 10 monsters that cannot be normal summoned, meaning it includes extra deck monsters and you can use monsters with the same name for the fusion.

Dark Magician/Diabellstar deck focusing on shooting out 2x Re-Eyes Dragoon.

Many different lines to get there but the ultimate combo is to give opponent Beelze with Fanatix Machinix, summon Assault Dragon, and then damage your oppenent to trigger an infinite loop - easiest way to damage is by summoning Nothung. Deck can consistently FTK and requires opponent to deploy hand traps at the exact precise time, as we generally have enough resources to survive an early Ash etc.

Going second plan is hope our hand traps keep us in the game and hope opponent doesn't stack an unbreakable board.

If the combo doesn't pan out (or if we mess something up), we can fall back into our individually strong extra deck threats like Hot Red, Dis Pater, Baronne, Full Armor Master, Assault Dragon, and Fanatix Machinix.

just a idea with the legendary modern deck "Mitsurugi" and the structure deck of RDA, i know it not perfect, i tried my best to make this deck budget and to put some hand traps and stay 40 cards in the main deck (any budget suggestions is open)

deck_name: Slifer, ruler of them all ! Summon slifer with his trap every game !
deck_excerpt: Slifer and Dragon Ruler deck. Don't wait to draw slifer, summon him with his trap like he deserve it.
deck_description:
Don't hesitate to tag me if you share the decklist ^^

↓↓↓ Combo + explanation below ↓↓↓

i'll add a video with combos soon.follow to not miss it.

Leave a comment, I'll be glad to help you ^^

 

DR = "dragon ruler" ( the whole engine or just the lvl7 )

bbDR = "baby dragon ruler"  ( the littles one )

>---------------------------------------------------<

How to summon Slifer the Sky Dragon with his The Revived Sky God 


-> Send Slifer the Sky Dragon  to the graveyard

From hand :

    Slifer the Sky Dragon  cannot be discard with dragon rulers's effects, so you have two solutions
        
        Dragon Ravine :

            It can be add by summoning Dragunity Knight - Romulus.
            Dragon Ravine  also allow you to foolish any dragon, that give you multiple way to extend your combos.

            -> Any dragon you foolish is a free target to banish to activate DR effects to summon themself, but you can optimise it :

                -> foolish a DR, then you can activate his effect from grave to banish 2 and summon him
                -> foolish a DR, then banish him with an other DR to activate his effect to add to hand a dragon type with the same attribute. It work really well with Tempest, Dragon Ruler of Storms  by giving you access to the whole "Armed dragon" engine.
                -> foolish a bbDR, banish him, summon Eclipse, Dragon Ruler of Catastrophes , activate his effect at summon to target the bb DR to special summon the DR with the same attribute to and shuffle the bb in the deck. 
                    That give you a level 7 on board who possibly hasn't use his effect so once you send him to the graveyard, you can summon him back with his effect or banish him to add one dragon with the same attribute from deck

                It's not useful to foolish the "Armed dragon" engine since you cannot activate their effect in hand, and you cannot get them back to hand.


        Muckraker From the Underworld :

            To activate muckraker effect to revive Lilith, Lady of Lament  you have to discard 1 card.

            Muckraker From the Underworld  is here for two reasons :

                -> special summon Lilith, Lady of Lament  from the graveyard to set The Revived Sky God 

                -> give a protection against destructions

            So if you drew Lilith, Lady of Lament  or the The Revived Sky God you don't need to summon Muckraker From the Underworld  to get the trap. 
                So you can still summon her to get destruction protection and the discard for Slifer the Sky Dragon .
                Or you can summon Dragunity Knight - Romulus  who add Dragon Ravine  from deck and is a dragon so he can be banish to activate DR effects, he can also summon dragon type monster from hand but give no protection.
 

From Deck : 

     - Use Chasma, Dragon Ruler of Auroras  effect to foolish Tidal, Dragon Ruler of Waterfalls  to foolish Slifer the Sky Dragon 

    Be careful of how much Tidal, Dragon Ruler of Waterfalls  you use in your combos, if you draw 2 Tidal, Dragon Ruler of Waterfalls  or 1 Tidal, Dragon Ruler of Waterfalls  and Stream, Dragon Ruler of Droplets , you can easyli get all your Tidal, Dragon Ruler of Waterfalls  out of your deck, 
        meaning you can't use his effect with Chasma, Dragon Ruler of Auroras , that why i play 3 of him.

    
>---------------------------------------------------<

-> Set The Revived Sky God  : 

Requirements :

-Cards : 
    
    Mecha Phantom Beast Dracossack 
    Cherubini, Ebon Angel of the Burning Abyss 
    Muckraker From the Underworld 
    Lilith, Lady of Lament 

-Combo :

 - Xyz Summon Mecha Phantom Beast Dracossack  with any two levels 7
 - Detach 1 from Mecha Phantom Beast Dracossack  to activate his effect to summon two tokens levels 3
 - Link summon Cherubini, Ebon Angel of the Burning Abyss  with the tokens
 - Activate Cherubini, Ebon Angel of the Burning Abyss  effect to foolish Lilith, Lady of Lament  and boost his attack
 - Link summon Muckraker From the Underworld  with Mecha Phantom Beast Dracossack  and Cherubini, Ebon Angel of the Burning Abyss 
 - Discard 1 to activate Muckraker From the Underworld  effect to summon Lilith, Lady of Lament  from the graveyard
 - Tribute Lilith, Lady of Lament  to activate her effect to reveal 3 The Revived Sky God  to set one

 And there it is, you have The Revived Sky God  on the field and Muckraker From the Underworld  can prevent destruction.

 There is games where you'll have Lilith, Lady of Lament  in hand, when this happen you can :

    -> Normal summon her, you rarely use your normal summon so it's not a problem.
    -> Discard her with Dragon Ravine  to summon her back with Muckraker From the Underworld 
    -> Discard her with Nebulus, Dragon Ruler of Mishaps 's effect and summon her back with Muckraker From the Underworld    



###########################################
Engine :
###########################################
>---------------------------------------------------<

-> Armed dragon engine :

    Armed dragon are WIND attributes dragon monsters, some of them share the particularity of activate their effect when they are
    send to the graveyard to activate a dragon type monster's effect, that include other dragon armed effect or bbDR's that require to discard a monster of the same attribute or a dragon type monster.
    They are WIND mean they can be discard to activate Tempest, Dragon Ruler of Storms  effect to add to your hand any dragon type monster (Spectral, Dragon Ruler of Flickers with any other DR is a full combo) .

    That mean they work really well with DR deck, but they also work well with the requirement of the Slifer engine.

    The Armed Dragon we use are level 7 or 3 that mean they can be used for XYZ summons Mecha Phantom Beast Dracossack  who start the combo, and the levels 3 can be use to Link summon Cherubini, Ebon Angel of the Burning Abyss , if you choose to summon Cherubini, Ebon Angel of the Burning Abyss  with Mecha Phantom Beast Dracossack 's effect, you can use those low levels dragon to summon Hieratic Seal of the Heavenly Spheres 

    Usually you play tenpaï with DR, but the tenpaï combo lock you in dragon type monster, so you cannot make the The Revived Sky God  combo.

Armed Dragon Flash  :


    Can be add to hand thank to Armed Dragon Thunder LV7 's effect.

    I choosed to play Armed Dragon Flash  instead of Armed Dragon Blitz  for 3 reasons :

        -> Armed Dragon Flash  doesn't lock you in dragon type, making it impossible to make the The Revived Sky God  searcher combo.

        -> Armed Dragon Flash  doesn't need a dragon armed on board to be used, you don't loose any value when you activate it.

        -> Armed Dragon Flash  has 3 excellent target target : Armed Dragon Thunder LV3  , Drill Armed Dragon  , Knight Armed Dragon, the Armored Knight Dragon 

Knight Armed Dragon, the Armored Knight Dragon :

    He can easily summon himself when a DR is summon.
    He can be summon thanks to Armed Dragon Flash 
    He is level 3 so he can be used to summon Cherubini, Ebon Angel of the Burning Abyss 
    He can boost a dragon by 1000, it happen to be useful ( you can pass Lunalight Liger Dancer  with Chasma, Dragon Ruler of Auroras  for example )    

Drill Armed Dragon  :

    The Goat !!!

    He can summon 3 different way :
        - normal summon ( you usually don't normal summon in DR archetype )
        - summoned Armed dragon LV3 by discarding a monster.
            -> That allow you to send another WIND dragon monster or a level 7 DR to activate Drill Armed Dragon  effect to banish Armed Dragon Thunder LV3 and the DR to add Spectral, Dragon Ruler of Flickers  and trigger the banished DR's effect to add one dragon with the same attributes that you'll be able to use for Spectral, Dragon Ruler of Flickers  effect

        - summoned with Armed Dragon Flash , in case you have no monster to discard for Armed Dragon LV3 's effect

    His effect of banishing dragons to add a dragon to hand is perfect for DR monsters, it mean you can add to you hand bbDR by banishing the big ones and activating their effect when they are banish.

Pile Armed Dragon  : 

    He can summon himself by sending a DR7 from the hand to the GY.
    Then he can foolish an "Armed dragon" monster ( this is the cost ! ), to boost attack of a monster on the field.
        - !!! The turn you activate this effect, only the monster with the boost can attack
        This way you can foolish Armed Dragon Thunder LV7  to activate his effect and add to your hand an "Armed dragon" card.

    And he is LV7 so you can use him to XYZ summon.

Armed Dragon Thunder LV3  :

    When he is send to the grave to activate a dragon's effect, you can draw 1. It's not a lot bot it's always nice to have cards in your hand for DR effects or for Dragon Ravine  and Muckraker From the Underworld effect

    He is the one you'll normal summon if you have it in hand

    He can summon Drill Armed Dragon  or Knight Armed Dragon, the Armored Knight Dragon , by sending himself to the grave with a monster in hand.

Armed Dragon Thunder LV7  :

    When he is send to the graveyard to activate a dragon's effect, you can add from your deck to your hand an "Armed Dragon" card,
    you'll usually get Armed Dragon Flash  or Pile Armed Dragon .
    If you already have Armed Dragon Flash  and Pile Armed Dragon , Knight Armed Dragon, the Armored Knight Dragon  is a good target too.

>---------------------------------------------------<

-> Handtraps : 

    I wanted to optimise the handtraps when you play first and when you draw with The Revived Sky God  , that why i choosed these one :

Effect Veiler  :

    He is LIGHT so you can discard him to activate Spectral, Dragon Ruler of Flickers 's effect.
    He is not a once per turn, so if you draw multiple copy of him with The Revived Sky God , you can use it multiple time.

Droll & Lock Bird  :

    He is WIND so you can discard him to activate Tempest, Dragon Ruler of Storms 's effect.
    The Revived Sky God  make you oppnent draw, it mean, if you have Droll & Lock Bird  at the end of The Revived Sky God  resolution, you can activate Droll & Lock Bird 

Fantastical Dragon Phantazmay  :

    He is a dragon type monster, he is lvl 7 and can allow you to manage your hand if your opponent summon a link monster.

Infinite Impermanence  :

    If you already have Lilith, Lady of Lament  and The Revived Sky God  in hand you can still use her effect by revealing the remaining The Revived Sky God  and Infinite Impermanence.

    You can take it off the decklist but in this situtation it would make Lilith, Lady of Lament  a garnet if you don't have the combo to add to you hand Spectral, Dragon Ruler of Flickers

deck_excerpt: Silent Puppet — The Wrath of the Sky Dragon
deck_description:
Strings does not duel of his own will. Acting as a silent puppet under the control of Marik, he serves as a vessel for a far more sinister power lurking behind the scenes. Without emotion or hesitation, Strings executes every move with mechanical precision, as if each card played was already decided long before the duel began. His strategy is simple yet terrifying: stall the battlefield, gather power, and summon a force beyond the reach of ordinary monsters.

That force is the legendary Slifer the Sky Dragon. Fueled by the cards in its master’s hand, Slifer grows into an overwhelming divine presence capable of crushing entire fields of monsters with a single roar of thunder. While opponents struggle to break through endless defenses and regenerating monsters, Strings patiently prepares the moment when the Sky Dragon descends. Once Slifer awakens, the duel transforms into a hopeless struggle against the wrath of a god.

This deck was built for Inferno Cyclone, a NeoGoat Special Event, focusing on WIND synergy and progressive field control through the Ultimate Insect evolution line.

As part of the event rules, the Side Deck monster — Sasuke Samurai #2 — functioned as the deck’s commander, meaning it could be Special Summoned during the Main Phase under Inferno Cyclone conditions, adding an extra layer of tactical flexibility and surprise.

The strategy revolves around reducing the opponent’s ATK with Ultimate Insect LV5 and LV7, turning every battle into an advantage. Combined with recruiters like Flying Kamakiri #1, Howling Insect, and Pinch Hopper, the deck maintains constant field presence while accelerating access to higher-level forms.

Desert Twister acts as a powerful mid-to-late game threat, capitalizing on the WIND-heavy graveyard.

https://neogoatformat.blogspot.com/2026/03/inferno-cyclone-preview-challenge.html

deck_name: Neo Goat - Inferno Cyclone - Yamata Burn
deck_description:
This deck was built for Inferno Cyclone, a NeoGoat Special Event, combining FIRE burn pressure with a unique draw engine centered around Yamata Dragon.

The core strategy revolves around successfully attacking with Yamata Dragon, allowing you to draw until you have five cards in hand. This effect fuels the deck’s main plan: immediately converting those cards into direct damage through burn effects like Tremendous Fire, Secret Barrel, Just Desserts, and Ceasefire.

Rather than maintaining long-term advantage, the deck operates in cycles — attack, refill the hand, and unleash all available burn damage in a single explosive turn.

To ensure Yamata Dragon connects, the deck includes tools like Enemy Controller and Stop Defense, forcing opposing monsters into Attack Position and creating openings for direct pressure. Meanwhile, Masked Dragon and Totem Dragon provide consistency and tribute setup, while Mausoleum of the Emperor enables faster access to high-level threats.

Additional pressure comes from Chain Energy and Dark Room of Nightmare, punishing the opponent for every action and amplifying each burn effect.

As part of the event rules, the Side Deck monster — Flame Ruler — functioned as the deck’s commander, allowing it to be Special Summoned during the Main Phase under Inferno Cyclone conditions, accelerating tribute plays and reinforcing the deck’s aggressive tempo.

This deck doesn’t aim to control the duel — it aims to compress it, turning each successful attack into a full reload and each turn into a burst of unavoidable damage.

https://neogoatformat.blogspot.com/2026/03/inferno-cyclone-preview-challenge.html

This deck was built for Inferno Cyclone, a NeoGoat Special Event, focusing on disruption, resource recycling, and exploiting the event’s unique attribute-based mechanics.

At its core, the strategy revolves around repeatedly reusing flip-effect monsters through Tsukuyomi and Night Assailant, generating constant value with cards like Magician of Faith, Mask of Darkness, 4-Starred Ladybug of Doom, and Princess of Tsurugi.

A key interaction comes from Scroll of Bewitchment, which allows you to change monster attributes to manipulate the event rules. By turning monsters into WIND, the deck can repeatedly return them to the hand, while forcing non-FIRE/WIND monsters into the Spell & Trap Zone. This creates a unique form of disruption while setting up additional pressure.

That pressure is converted into damage through Princess of Tsurugi, which punishes the opponent for each Spell & Trap card they control — including monsters that have been forced into those zones by the event mechanics.

The deck also features strong hand-loop and recursion plays. The Tricky can be Special Summoned by discarding Night Assailant, which in turn recovers a Flip monster, creating continuous advantage and enabling repeated control sequences.

Control tools like Phoenix Wing Wind Blast, Anti-Spell Fragrance, Spatial Collapse, and Xing Zhen Hu further restrict the opponent’s ability to play, while Ultimate Offering allows sudden tempo swings when needed.

As part of the event rules, the Side Deck monster — Sasuke Samurai — functioned as the deck’s commander, allowing it to be Special Summoned during the Main Phase under Inferno Cyclone conditions, adding precision removal and extra pressure.

 

This deck doesn’t aim to overpower the opponent — it reshapes the duel itself, turning field control, hand loops, and event mechanics into a continuous engine of disruption and burn.

https://neogoatformat.blogspot.com/2026/03/inferno-cyclone-preview-challenge.html

deck_name: Neo Goat - Inferno Cyclone - Wind Riders
deck_description:
This deck was built for Inferno Cyclone, a NeoGoat Special Event, centered around enabling Chaos Greed through full graveyard removal across monsters, spells, and traps.

The core strategy is to keep the Graveyard empty, allowing Chaos Greed to be activated consistently for massive draw power. Cards like Banisher of the Light prevent cards from ever reaching the Graveyard, while D.D. Warrior Lady, D.D. Assailant, Exiled Force, and Chaosrider Gustaph actively remove threats from play.

Different Dimension Capsule plays a key role by banishing cards directly from the deck without interacting with the Graveyard, helping maintain the conditions required for Chaos Greed while setting up future plays.

Book of Moon serves a dual purpose: it acts as a defensive tool while also enabling the reuse of removal effects by resetting monsters, allowing their banish effects to be applied again and extending the deck’s control over the Graveyard.

Once the Graveyard is cleared, Chaos Greed becomes a powerful engine, refilling the hand and enabling continuous pressure. Cards like Pot of Greed and Upstart Goblin further accelerate access to key pieces.

Bazoo the Soul-Eater converts banished monsters into raw attack power, while Diskblade Rider maintains offensive pressure within the removal-based strategy.

The deck can convert this advantage into explosive turns with Return from the Different Dimension, bringing back multiple banished monsters at once for a decisive push.

As part of the event rules, the Side Deck monster — Chaosrider Gustaph — functioned as the deck’s commander, allowing it to be Special Summoned during the Main Phase under Inferno Cyclone conditions, reinforcing the deck’s removal engine and ensuring Chaos Greed remains active.

 

This deck doesn’t just remove cards — it erases the Graveyard entirely, turning that absence into overwhelming card advantage.

https://neogoatformat.blogspot.com/2026/03/inferno-cyclone-preview-challenge.html

deck_name: Neo Goat - Inferno Cyclone  - Ninja Phoenix
deck_description:
This deck was built for Inferno Cyclone, a NeoGoat Special Event, focusing on FIRE synergy and recursive destruction through Sacred Phoenix of Nephthys.

The strategy revolves around triggering Phoenix consistently with Circle of the Fire Kings, allowing it to return and wipe Spell & Trap cards while maintaining pressure.

Flame Armor Ninja plays a key role by becoming Level 5 when summoned, enabling direct access to Phoenix through Ninjitsu Art of Transformation, while also serving as material for Metamorphosis to summon Level 5 Fusion monsters.

Lonefire Blossom is used to send FIRE monsters to the Graveyard, enabling Spirit of Flames for fast field presence and tempo plays.

With additional support from Metamorphosis and control cards, the deck creates constant field resets while sustaining pressure through recursion.

 

As part of the event rules, the Side Deck monster — Little Chimera — functioned as the deck’s commander, allowing it to be Special Summoned during the Main Phase under Inferno Cyclone conditions, boosting FIRE monsters and reinforcing the deck’s aggressive playstyle.

https://neogoatformat.blogspot.com/2026/03/inferno-cyclone-preview-challenge.html

deck_name: Neo Goat - Inferno Cyclone - Fire Princess
deck_description:
This deck was built for Inferno Cyclone, a NeoGoat Special Event, focusing on Life Point manipulation and burn damage through Fire Princess.

The core strategy revolves around repeatedly gaining Life Points with cards like Solemn Wishes, Goblin Thief, and Good Goblin Housekeeping, triggering Fire Princess to convert that gain into direct damage.

Elemental HERO Prisma plays a key role by revealing St. Joan to send Darklord Marie to the Graveyard, enabling continuous Life Point gain and reinforcing the burn engine.

Volcanic Shell provides constant discard fuel for Raigeki Break and Good Goblin Housekeeping, maintaining hand flow and enabling repeated plays.

Stall tools like Messenger of Peace, Gravity Bind, and Level Limit - Area B slow the duel down, giving the deck time to build its engine and accumulate damage safely.

As part of the event rules, the Side Deck monster — Fire Princess — functioned as the deck’s commander, allowing it to be Special Summoned during the Main Phase under Inferno Cyclone conditions, accelerating the deck’s win condition.

 

This deck doesn’t rely on battle — it transforms Life Point gain into a constant source of damage, turning recovery into inevitability.

https://neogoatformat.blogspot.com/2026/03/inferno-cyclone-preview-challenge.html

This deck was built for Inferno Cyclone, a NeoGoat Special Event, focusing on WIND tempo and continuous backrow removal through Harpies' Hunting Ground.

The strategy revolves around repeatedly triggering Hunting Ground, turning every Harpie summon into Spell and Trap destruction. With Harpie Queen searching the field spell and Elegant Egotist enabling swarming, the deck maintains constant pressure while clearing the opponent’s backrow.

Cards like Flying Kamakiri #1 and Birdface provide consistency, while Swallow’s Nest allows quick transformations and protects key monsters during combat.

Sky Scout supports the strategy by maintaining hand flow, while Icarus Attack converts field presence into powerful removal.

As part of the event rules, the Side Deck monster — Harpie’s Pet Baby Dragon — functioned as the deck’s commander, allowing it to be Special Summoned during the Main Phase under Inferno Cyclone conditions, reinforcing the deck’s aggressive tempo.

 

This deck doesn’t just attack — it dismantles the opponent’s field piece by piece, turning every summon into disruption.

deck_name: Neo Goat - Inferno Cyclone - Volcanic Horus
deck_excerpt: Lock the duel. Burn what remains.
deck_description:
This deck was built for Inferno Cyclone, a NeoGoat Special Event, combining FIRE pressure with the spell-negation lock of Horus the Black Flame Dragon.

The strategy focuses on advancing through the Horus levels using Masked Dragon, UFO Turtle, and FIRE support monsters, quickly establishing Horus LV6 and eventually LV8, shutting down Spell cards entirely.

The FIRE engine, powered by Volcanic Shell, provides constant discard fuel for Raigeki Break and maintains hand advantage, while Circle of the Fire Kings helps trigger destruction effects and extend plays.

Cards like Book of Moon protect Horus during its progression, ensuring it can safely reach its higher forms and maintain control of the duel.

As part of the event rules, the Side Deck monster — A-Team: Trap Disposal Unit — functioned as the deck’s commander, allowing it to be Special Summoned during the Main Phase under Inferno Cyclone conditions, providing additional support.

 

Once Horus is established, the duel shifts completely — spells become useless, and the opponent is forced to play under constant pressure from FIRE-based threats.

https://neogoatformat.blogspot.com/2026/03/inferno-cyclone-preview-challenge.html

1-card combo tutorial - Sky Striker Mobilize - Engage! 
- activate Engage, add Pillar of the Future - Cyanos 
- normal summon Cyanos, effect to summon Sky Striker Ace - Roze from deck
- Link Roze into Sky Striker Ace - Kagari, effect on summon to add Engage back to hand
- Link Cyanos and Kagari into R.B. VALCan Booster, (summon to RIGHT EMZ) effect on summon to add R.B. Funk Dock 
- activate Engage again to add Sky Striker Mecha - Widow Anchor
- activate Funk Dock, add R.B. Stage Landing 
- activate Stage Landing, summon R.B. Lambda Cannon from deck to zone below Booster
- activate Lambda Cannon's effect: pay 1200LP, destroy itself, check your opponent's hand, summon a monster there to their field with effects negated (if they have Nibiru, choose Nibiru)
- Lambda Cannon leaving the field triggers Funk Dock, summon R.B. Lambda Blade from deck to zone below Booster
- Lambda Blade effect on summon, dump R.B. Ga10 Driller from deck
- activate Booster effect, summon Lambda Cannon from GY and move Booster out of EMZ
- Lambda Cannon effect on summon to add Driller from GY to hand
- Link Booster and Cannon into R.B. The Brute Blues into the RIGHT EMZ
- activate Brute Blues effect to add Stage Landing, activate Stage Landing to summon R.B. Shepherd's Crook to centre MMZ
- activate Shepherd's Crook to set R.B. Next Phase directly from deck.
- special summon Driller from hand to zone on the left of Shepherd's Crook
- activate Cyanos in the GY, banish it to summon Roze from GY
- Link Roze into Sky Striker Ace - Shizuku 
- set Widow Anchor
- End phase - Shizuku effect to add Sky Striker Mecha - Hornet Drones for follow-up

End Board
- Shepherd's Crook: can (quick effect) return 2 R.B.s form GY to deck and summon a 3rd, 3500ATK (1500 + 4 x 500)
- Brute Blues: can't be destroyed, 6400ATK (1500 +3500 (Shepherd's Crook) + 1400 (Lambda Blade))
- Lambda Blade: while next to R.B. arrow, (quick effect) pay 1400LP, pop itself, snatch steal (destroy during end phase)
- Driller: while next to R.B. arrow, (quick effect) pay 500LP, target and pop 1 monster your opponent controls and itself
- Shizuku: reduce opponent monsters ATK by 100 x number of spells in your GY
- Funk Dock: when an R.B. leaves your field, summon another from deck (for during opponent's turn, choose R.B. Ga10 Cutter , Driller, or Lambda Blade)
- Next Phase: counter trap monster effect negate + gain 2000LP, cost of destroying 1 monster you control
- 2x Stage Landing in GY: banish to protect your R.B. monsters from being destroyed (sometimes by their own effects)
- Widow Anchor: just in case

TIP: if you summon Lambda Blade again on your opponent's turn using Funk Dock or something similar, use its effect to dump R.B. Last Stand from your deck - you can banish it to protect your R.B.s from being targeted once

deck_description:
The ladder police are on the prowl for warcrimes, stun strategies and floodgates... Crystron K9!
 
While Vanquish Soul is the more popular choice to pair up with K9, Crystron also has immense synergy with K9! Both strategies can easily spam out level 5s for XYZ/Synchro summons and either archetype can pivot into the other. Simultaneously raising the power ceiling and consistency of Crystron, K9 offers extension when interrupted, insulation from handtraps, board clearance, and extra ways to access the Crystron engine! As a result of this consistency boost, the Crystron engine has transformed into a ultra compact set of cards! On top of that, Crystron turbos out Spell Canceller,  so your opponent cannot use any spell cards! Wielding more non-engine than ever and packing a perfect support archetype, Crystron remains meta relevant as it carves a niche for itself in the 2026 Masterduel metagame!

deck_excerpt: A more engine-forward version of the Blue Eyes Spirit deck
deck_description: A more engine-forward version of the Blue Eyes Spirit deck

INTRODUCTION:

 

This deck is a showcasing of the Psychic cards, a set of cards that function like an archetype, but aren’t one due to the logistics of having an archetype with the same name as a subtype, much less one that interacts heavily WITH said subtype. The deck is a combo deck, mainly focused on large synchro plays and abusing Emergency Teleport almost as much as TeleD.A.D did. Below is a semi-comprehensive guide to the lines and playstyle of this deck, and many videos exist online of others explaining them more in-depth than I can here.

 

COMBO GUIDE:

 

FULL-COMBO ENDBOARD:

 

1x Psychic End Punisher

1x Hyper Psychic Riser

1x PSY-Framelord Omega

 

FULL-COMBO LINES:

 

NOTE: > Indicates when a card searches another card from the deck.

[] Indicate actions that happen throughout the line that do not involve searching or synchro summoning a new card

 

LINE 1: 

 

NEEDED: 1x Serene Psychic Girl

 

Serene Psychic Girl > Mind Procedure > Emergency Teleport > Virtual World Hime - Nyannyan 

 

Mind Procedure + Virtual World Hime - Nyannyan = Serene Psychic Sorceress  > Emergency Teleport

 

Serene Psychic Girl + Serene Psychic Sorceress = P.U.N.K JAM Dragon Drive [JAM Dragon get Minister to hand] [Minister special summon]

 

Emergency Teleport > Noh-P.U.N.K Ze Amin [Nyannyan special summon] > Noh-P.U.N.K Foxy Tune (send to GY + 1 card from hand) > Noh-P.U.N.K Deer Note 

 

P.U.N.K JAM Dragon Drive + Virtual World Hime - Nyannyan = Psychic End Punisher

 

Noh-P.U.N.K Ze Amin + Noh-P.U.N.K Deer Note = PSY-Framelord Omega [Deer Note special summon Ze Amin]

 

Noh-P.U.N.K Ze Amin 3 + Hushed Psychic Minister 3 = Hyper Psychic Riser

 

LINE 2:

 

NEEDED: 1x Mind Procedure

 

Mind Procedure > Emergency Teleport > Serene Psychic Girl > Virtual World Hime - Nyannyan

 

Mind Procedure + Virtual World Hime - Nyannyan = Serene Psychic Sorceress  > Emergency Teleport

 

Serene Psychic Girl + Serene Psychic Sorceress = P.U.N.K JAM Dragon Drive [JAM Dragon get Minister to hand] [Minister special summon]

 

Emergency Teleport > Noh-P.U.N.K Ze Amin [Nyannyan special summon] > Noh-P.U.N.K Foxy Tune (send to GY + 1 card from hand) > Noh-P.U.N.K Deer Note 

 

P.U.N.K JAM Dragon Drive + Virtual World Hime - Nyannyan = Psychic End Punisher

 

Noh-P.U.N.K Ze Amin + Noh-P.U.N.K Deer Note = PSY-Framelord Omega [Deer Note special summon Ze Amin]

 

Noh-P.U.N.K Ze Amin 3 + Hushed Psychic Minister 3 = Hyper Psychic Riser

 

PLAYING THROUGH INTERRUPTION:

 

This deck is a bare-bones combo deck, meaning it cannot play through hand traps well. Often, a hand trap at any point throughout the combo will put it to a complete stop. The deck runs Called by the Grave and Crossout Designator as its most direct counters. Psi-Blocker can be used as well, and is easy to get on the field using Serene Psychic Girl, but clogs up the combo, preventing a full endboard. A Psi-Blocker line typically plays out as follows:

 

Serene Psychic Girl > Psi-Blocker [Psi-Blocker declares a hand trap like Ash Blossom, Ghost Belle, etc.] [Hope and pray that it works]

 

Serene Psychic Girl + Psi-Blocker = Serene Psychic Sorceress

 

Serene Psychic Sorceress > Emergency Teleport

 

Emergency Teleport > Noh-P.U.N.K Ze Amin [Nyannyan special summon] > Noh-P.U.N.K Foxy Tune (send to GY + 1 card from hand) > Noh-P.U.N.K Deer Note 

 

Noh-P.U.N.K Ze Amin 3 + Noh-P.U.N.K Deer Note = P.U.N.K JAM Dragon Drive [Deer Note special summon Ze Amin]

 

P.U.N.K JAM Dragon Drive + Noh-P.U.N.K Ze Amin = Psychic End Punisher

 

Cards in the deck like Psychic Tracker and Psychic Wheeleder can help bolster the deck's resilience, but still cannot guarantee full combo with a starter in hand. For example:

 

Serene Psychic Girl [NEGATED] [Psychic Tracker special summon]

 

Your ONLY two options in the entirety of Yu-Gi-Oh to play here are Magical Android (in the deck for this reason), the completely useless in this deck PSY-Frame Lambda, and the completely useless in this deck Daigusto Gulldos. Feel free to put some PSY-Frame cards like Gamma and Delta to facilitate Lambda, but I’ve found it doesn’t help the problem much.

 

In the case of starting with Mind Procedure, the outlook is a little less grim:

 

Mind Procedure [NEGATED] [Psychic Tracker special summon]

 

Mind Procedure + Psychic Tracker = Serene Psychic Sorceress

 

Serene Psychic Sorceress > Emergency Teleport

 

Emergency Teleport > Noh-P.U.N.K Ze Amin [Nyannyan special summon] > Noh-P.U.N.K Foxy Tune (send to GY + 1 card from hand) > Noh-P.U.N.K Deer Note 

 

Noh-P.U.N.K Ze Amin 3 + Noh-P.U.N.K Deer Note = P.U.N.K JAM Dragon Drive [Deer Note special summon Ze Amin]

 

P.U.N.K JAM Dragon Drive + Noh-P.U.N.K Ze Amin = Psychic End Punisher

 

ENDNOTES:

 

It’s important to note that the Psychic strategy as it is now, is a very fragile and very fun, albeit gimmicky combo deck. Despite the fact that cards like Psychic Tracker and Psychic Wheeleder see play as extenders in Rank-3 focused strategies like Phantom Knights (getting great new support soon LETS GO), the cards remain a non-archetype, likely due to the confusion around having an archetype that shares a name with a subtype, which many of the cards IN the archetype coincidentally mention. This deck is meant to serve as a proof of concept that Psychic as a strategy can be turned into a deck, less than a fully optimized list. Regardless, I’ve had loads of fun building and playing this deck! I hope you enjoy it as much as I have :).

 

THANKS FOR READING! (it was long I know)

deck_excerpt: Twin Shadows — The Masked Beast Awakens
deck_description:
Umbra and Lumis do not duel as individuals, but as a single, calculating entity hidden behind masks and mystery. Speaking in unison and moving with eerie coordination, the masked duo turns every duel into a psychological trap. Their strategy is built on restriction and control, sealing away their opponent’s strongest plays while slowly tightening their grip on the battlefield. Against them, power alone is meaningless — because they decide what you are allowed to summon.

Behind their oppressive tactics lies their true weapon: The Masked Beast. Summoned through dark ritual magic, this terrifying entity emerges once the duel has been completely locked down, ready to deliver the final blow. With masks that weaken, restrict, and manipulate, Umbra and Lumis transform the duel into a suffocating maze where escape is impossible. By the time their opponent realizes what’s happening, the beast has already awakened… and the end is inevitable.

Pretty dangerous deck that plays pretty well into the meta (jan-mar 2026 master duel).

Goal is pretty straight forward:make an hard to break endboard (gryphon+a bao+desirae+ki-sikil to revive lil-la+sometimes caesar+ backrow/handtrap) then outgrind the opponent if they manage to break through the board.

Backrows can be a problem for this deck that's why i forced in Duster (you can guess that Dracotail and striker are not easy fights) but the biggest counter is Super poly which outs half of your board for free (or all of It if they have two).

Besides that the deck Is actually really solid, maybe password 2x isnt a neccessity but i play It anyway.
I am trying to fix the deck to make it the best way possible when the card come out in tcg

deck_excerpt: What draws 7 cards, plays turn 0, and has 14 handtraps in GENESYS? URSARCTIC MITSURUGI?!
deck_description:
What strategy draws 7 cards, plays turn 0, has infinite grindgame, can go +20 on turn 1 and has 14 handtraps? It's not some crazy meta deck, it's URSARCTIC MITSURUGI!

These bears n' adders work surprisingly well together, since all Ursarctic does is tribute level 7 or higher monsters, and the Mitsurugi rituals are level 8! Whenever they're tributed, you get to search any Mitsurugi combo piece from deck, meaning you can theoretically search everything you need to play Mitsurugi on turn 0 while interrupting your opponent with massed Ursarctic handtraps!

Not yet impressed? Ursarctic sports a DRAW SEVEN continuous spell - Ursarctic Radiation as it's strongest card, allowing you draw into more advantage like side deck cards, Maxx "C," Mitsurugi engine, etc. All the level 8 Ursarctic monsters are handtraps, with big clawed Megabilis (D.D Crow), hammer wielding Megapolar (MST) & the bow sniping Megatanus (Book of Moon). Ursarctic Septentrion made with Polar Star makes an incredibly oppressive 3K attack floodgate, rendering any Extra Deck monster weak and helpless vs. your bear and reptile onslaught. 

I chose to spend all GENESYS points on this deck on double Maxx "C," since there's basically no room on non-engine, so I took the most expensive high impact card I can get that obtains more advantage, since this strategy thrives on drawing and searching 10+ cards. After you control your opponent's deck with your Ursarctic cards, wipe their field with Mitsurugi and drown them in card advantage with both decks, you should be able to win! 

GLHF Duelists!
